title: Propidium Iodide (PI) Staining Method
author: Dr. Steven Wilhelm
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.ibxcapn

steps.title: Make a sample by adding 25%...
steps.instruction: Make a sample by adding 25% glycerol to cells Samples can be stored at -20ºC for 30 days. Glycerol growcells.com Catalog #MRGE-4002

steps.title: Add DAPI at 50 µg/mL in dH 2 O
steps.instruction: Add DAPI at 50 µg/mL in dH 2 O

steps.title: Add PI at 500 µg/mL in dH 2 O
steps.instruction: Add PI at 500 µg/mL in dH 2 O

steps.title: Filter sterilize with 0
steps.instruction: Filter sterilize with 0.2 µm Millipore filter and store in the dark at 4ºC

steps.title: Dilute cells appropriately according to your sample
steps.instruction: Dilute cells appropriately according to your sample

steps.title: Add DAPI at a final concentration of 5 µg/mL
steps.instruction: Add DAPI at a final concentration of 5 µg/mL

steps.title: Add PI at a final concentration of 3 µg/mL
steps.instruction: Add PI at a final concentration of 3 µg/mL

steps.title: Incubate in the dark for 30 min
steps.instruction: Incubate in the dark for 30 min

steps.title: Filter sample onto a 0
steps.instruction: Filter sample onto a 0.2 µm black polycarbonate filter under low vacuum (<15 mmHg)

steps.title: Mount the filter onto a glass...
steps.instruction: Mount the filter onto a glass slide with low fluorescence immersion oil

steps.title: View the sample under the Leica...
steps.instruction: View the sample under the Leica DM4000-6000 epifluorescent microscope under the Texas Red N3 filter at green excitiation = 546 nm and emission = 600 nm
